description_long: This is a small, fixed-wing airplane with a weathered yellow and black paint scheme, featuring a single propeller mounted at the nose. The fuselage is compact and boxy, with two circular windows on each side and a cockpit at the front. The wings are straight with a slight dihedral angle and are mounted low on the fuselage, extending outward with squared tips. The tail section includes a conventional vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers, both showing signs of wear and chipped paint. The landing gear consists of two main wheels under the wings and a smaller nose wheel, all fixed and exposed. The surface appears metallic and rugged, suggesting a utilitarian or vintage design suitable for display or light utility use.
description: A small, weathered yellow and black propeller airplane with a boxy fuselage, low-mounted straight wings, and fixed landing gear. The surface is rugged and metallic, with visible wear and chipping.
description_view_0: Front view showing the single nose-mounted propeller, cockpit windows, and the broad, low-mounted wings with visible wear.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the two circular windows, fixed landing gear, and the weathered paint along the fuselage and wing.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the tail section with a conventional vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers, as well as the full wingspan.
description_view_3: Right side view similar to the left, showing the propeller, windows, and fixed landing gear, with distinct paint chipping.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Low-wing configuration with straight wings and a slight dihedral angle, no visible winglets or sweep.
attribute_engine_type: Single nose-mounted piston engine with a two-blade propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and two horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Fixed tricycle landing gear with two main wheels and one nose wheel.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit with side windows, likely single or dual seat.
